methodology_sections: 1.1 Asset Inventory - Financial assets, state assets, privilege assets, reputation assets
1.3 System Architecture Mapping - Contract hierarchies, dependencies, upgrade patterns, access control
2.1 Entry Point Enumeration - Public/external functions, callbacks, parameter analysis

financial_assets: ERC20 collateral tokens held in DegenBox/BentoBox shares (DegenBox.balanceOf[token][user], totals[token])
MIM (magicInternetMoney) balances/shares inside BentoBox and CauldronV4 (borrowed principal + interest + fees)
ERC20 base/quote reserves held by MagicLP/BlastMagicLP pools (base/quote token balances backing LP supply)
MagicLP LP tokens (ERC20 shares) representing pool ownership; also used as stakingToken in LockingMultiRewards
Flash-loanable ERC20 liquidity in DegenBox (flashLoan/batchFlashLoan) and MagicLP (flashLoan)
Protocol fee flows: CauldronV4 accrueInfo.feesEarned (interest+fees) withdrawable to feeTo; MagicLP maintainer fee transfers via IFeeRateModel.maintainer()
Blast native yield streams (gas + ETH yield) claimable via BlastYields for various wrapped contracts and pools
Blast rebasing token yields (e.g., WETH/USDB on Blast) claimable to feeTo for BlastBox/BlastMagicLP/BlastOnboarding/BlastGovernor
BlastOnboarding deposits: supportedTokens balances tracked (locked/unlocked) and physically held ERC20s in BlastOnboarding proxy
LockingMultiRewards reward tokens funded via notifyRewardAmount and held by contract; user reward locks represent delayed-claim balances
state_assets: CauldronV4: totalBorrow (Rebase elastic/base), totalCollateralShare, exchangeRate cache, accrueInfo (lastAccrued, feesEarned, interestPerSecond)
CauldronV4: userCollateralShare[user], userBorrowPart[user], borrowLimit caps, blacklistedCallees allowlist/denylist for cook ACTION_CALL

MagicLP/BlastMagicLP: reserves/targets (_BASE_RESERVE_, _QUOTE_RESERVE_, _BASE_TARGET_, _QUOTE_TARGET_), pricing params (_I_, _K_), fee params (_LP_FEE_RATE_, _MT_FEE_RATE_MODEL_), TWAP accumulator (_BASE_PRICE_CUMULATIVE_LAST_, _BLOCK_TIMESTAMP_LAST_), initialization flag
Factory: implementation address, maintainerFeeRateModel, pools[base][quote] registry arrays, userPools[creator] registry arrays
Router: immutable weth/factory; no persistent balances intended but can temporarily hold WETH/ETH during liquidity removal/swap flows

LockingMultiRewards: per-reward-token reward schedule and accumulators, per-user balances (locked/unlocked), locks arrays, reward locks arrays, rewardTokens list, supplies, stakingTokenBalance, minLockAmount, lastLockIndex
BlastOnboarding: state machine (Idle/Opened/Closed), supportedTokens, caps, totals per token, per-user balances, bootstrapper (proxy implementation pointer), feeTo, registry
BlastOnboardingBoot: pool, router/factory pointers, totalPoolShares, ready flag, staking contract, claimed[user]
privilege_assets: Ownership (solmate Owned) across: CauldronV4, MagicLP/BlastMagicLP, Factory, ProxyOracle, BlastOnboarding(+Data), BlastOnboardingBootDataV1, BlastTokenRegistry; plus OperatableV2/V3 operator sets
CauldronV4 masterContract owner authority via onlyMasterContractOwner: interest rate, borrow limits, blacklist, liquidation/borrow fee params, reduceSupply
DegenBox (via MasterContractManager/BoringOwnable): owner controls strategy parameters, strategy assignment, whitelist master contracts, approvals logic
BlastBox: onlyOwner can enable deposit tokens and set feeTo; onlyOperators can claim yields
BlastMagicLP: implementation owner controls feeTo and operator set (implementation-only); clone calls restricted to implementation operators/owner
BlastOnboarding: onlyOwner controls lifecycle open/close, supported tokens/caps, bootstrapper implementation pointer, pause/unpause, yield claiming, rescue for non-supported tokens
BlastGovernor: onlyOwner can execute arbitrary calls and precompile calls; onlyOperators can claim yields to feeTo
reputation_assets: LP share supply integrity (MagicLP ERC20 totalSupply and accounting); TWAP accumulator used by integrators
Oracle integrity: ProxyOracle and MagicLpAggregator answers used for collateral valuation/markets
Staking positions and reward accounting correctness (LockingMultiRewards locks and reward locks)
Cauldron solvency/position health correctness impacting liquidation fairness and protocol trust
Factory registry correctness (pools/userPools arrays) used by UIs/indexers for discovery
Blast points/yield configuration for protocol contracts (governor attribution / points operator)

system_architecture.inheritance_hierarchy: CauldronV4 inherits solmate Owned and IMasterContract (clone initialization). MagicLP inherits solady ERC20 + ReentrancyGuard + solmate Owned; BlastMagicLP inherits MagicLP and adds Blast yield/operator controls. LockingMultiRewards inherits OperatableV2 (solmate Owned) and OZ Pausable. Factory inherits solmate Owned and deploys deterministic clones of MagicLP implementation via LibClone. Router is standalone periphery interacting with MagicLP/Factory and WETH. DegenBox inherits MasterContractManager (BoringOwnable+BoringFactory) and BoringBatchable; BlastBox inherits DegenBox and OperatableV3 (custom operator mgmt) and overrides owner check. BlastOnboardingData inherits solmate Owned + OZ Pausable; BlastOnboarding inherits BlastOnboardingData + OZ Proxy (delegates to bootstrapper). BlastOnboardingBootDataV1 extends BlastOnboardingData and adds new storage; BlastOnboardingBoot extends that (meant to be used via BlastOnboarding proxy delegatecall). BlastWrappers deploy wrapped Router/Factory/Cauldron that configure Blast yields. ProxyOracle inherits solmate Owned and implements IOracle by delegating to oracleImplementation. BlastGovernor inherits OperatableV2 and can execute arbitrary calls as owner.
external_dependencies: Solady: SafeTransferLib, ReentrancyGuard, ERC20, SafeCastLib, LibClone, FixedPointMathLib
OpenZeppelin: Proxy, Pausable, Address, IERC20/IERC20Metadata interfaces
BoringSolidity: BoringMath, BoringRebase/RebaseLibrary, BoringERC20, BoringOwnable, BoringFactory, BoringBatchable
Blast precompiles/interfaces: IBlast, IERC20Rebasing, IBlastPoints; BlastYields and BlastPoints libraries
Custom MIMSwap libs: PMMPricing, DecimalMath, Math
Protocol interfaces: IBentoBoxV1, ISwapperV2, IOracle, IFeeRateModel, IStrategy, IMagicLP, IFactory, IAggregator
system_architecture.upgrade_patterns: Multiple patterns coexist: (1) Clone/factory pattern for MagicLP pools (LibClone.cloneDeterministic) with init() guard flag. (2) MasterContract/clone pattern for CauldronV4 via IMasterContract init() and masterContract immutable pointing to implementation. (3) Upgradeable proxy for BlastOnboarding using OpenZeppelin Proxy delegating to bootstrapper; storage lives in proxy (BlastOnboarding/BlastOnboardingData/BootData). No UUPS/Transparent admin slots shown; it is a minimal delegate proxy controlled by owner via setBootstrapper().
system_architecture.access_control_structure: Predominantly single-owner (solmate Owned / BoringOwnable) with additional operator roles (OperatableV2/V3) for yield claims and reward distribution. CauldronV4 has dual authority: clone owner (onlyOwner) for feeTo; masterContract.owner() for risk parameters via onlyMasterContractOwner. BlastMagicLP splits authority: clones can call yield-claim methods only if caller is implementation operator/owner; implementation owner manages operator set and feeTo. BlastOnboarding proxy owner controls state machine, bootstrapper (implementation address), supported tokens, caps, pausing, and yield claiming. DegenBox/MasterContractManager uses owner for whitelist/strategy management and EIP-712 approvals for master contracts.

state_changes: Multiple: collateral/borrow state, approvals, totals, and arbitrary side effects depending on ACTION_CALL and BentoBox actions
Same as withdraw + rewards reset/locks updated
external_calls: rewardToken.safeTransfer(msg.sender, amount) (for matured reward lock items during _getRewards)
rewardToken.safeTransfer(msg.sender, amount) (only when prior lock expired)

entry_points.authentication_required: allowed(from) requires either msg.sender==from OR skimming from BentoBox OR approved masterContract clone
allowed(from) requires either msg.sender==from OR skimming from BentoBox OR approved masterContract clone
external_calls: IWETH.withdraw(amount) + ETH call to to (ETH path)
token.safeTransfer(strategy, amountOut) (during strategy divest/invest in harvest, not here)

initializer_guards: None (constructor sets registry/feeTo; proxy delegates based on bootstrapper address; no explicit initializer guard for delegated implementation)
upgradeability_surface.notes: Custom OZ Proxy with owner-controlled setBootstrapper(). Storage lives in proxy (BlastOnboardingData + BootDataV1). Upgrading bootstrapper without strict storage layout/versioning can corrupt balances/state. Author comment indicates storage collision risk.
Clone pattern via Factory+LibClone. Not proxy-upgradeable per clone; implementation can be swapped in Factory for new clones only.
MasterContract/clone initialization pattern; masterContract immutable points to implementation; per-clone state initialized once.
Non-upgradeable vault; extensibility via strategies and master contract approvals.
Not a proxy; mutable oracleImplementation pointer set by owner.
Owner can change implementation used for future clones (not an upgrade of existing pools).
Not upgradeable; owner has arbitrary execute().
